region of [New South Wales](/wiki/New_South_Wales "New South Wales"), Australia, refers generally to the area west of the [Northern Tablelands](/wiki/Northern_Tablelands "Northern Tablelands"), to the north of the [Central West](/wiki/Central_West_(New_South_Wales) "Central West (New South Wales)") region and to the east of the [Far West](/wiki/Far_West_(New_South_Wales) "Far West (New South Wales)") region. Despite its name, the region is in north-central New South Wales, corresponding generally to the Australian [Bureau of Meteorology](/wiki/Bureau_of_Meteorology "Bureau of Meteorology")'s forecast area of *North West Slopes and Plains*. The administrative areas of the region include the city of [Tamworth](/wiki/Tamworth,_New_South_Wales "Tamworth, New South Wales"), [Gunnedah](/wiki/Gunnedah,_New_South_Wales "Gunnedah, New South Wales"), [Moree](/wiki/Moree,_New_South_Wales "Moree, New South Wales"), [Narrabri](/wiki/Narrabri,_New_South_Wales "Narrabri, New South Wales") and [Inverell](/wiki/Inverell,_New_South_Wales "Inverell, New South Wales").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Region in New South Wales, Australia

Region in New South Wales, Australia

The **North West Slopes** region of [New South Wales](/wiki/New_South_Wales "New South Wales"), Australia, refers generally to the area west of the [Northern Tablelands](/wiki/Northern_Tablelands "Northern Tablelands"), to the north of the [Central West](/wiki/Central_West_(New_South_Wales) "Central West (New South Wales)") region and to the east of the [Far West](/wiki/Far_West_(New_South_Wales) "Far West (New South Wales)") region. Despite its name, the region is in north-central New South Wales, corresponding generally to the Australian [Bureau of Meteorology](/wiki/Bureau_of_Meteorology "Bureau of Meteorology")'s forecast area of *North West Slopes and Plains*. The administrative areas of the region include the city of [Tamworth](/wiki/Tamworth,_New_South_Wales "Tamworth, New South Wales"), [Gunnedah](/wiki/Gunnedah,_New_South_Wales "Gunnedah, New South Wales"), [Moree](/wiki/Moree,_New_South_Wales "Moree, New South Wales"), [Narrabri](/wiki/Narrabri,_New_South_Wales "Narrabri, New South Wales") and [Inverell](/wiki/Inverell,_New_South_Wales "Inverell, New South Wales").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

The region is higher, hillier and wetter on its eastern edge than on its western edge; with the exception of the steeply-rising [Nandewar Range](/wiki/Nandewar_Range "Nandewar Range") which lies in the west of the region. The North West Slopes are situated on various tributaries to the [Darling River](/wiki/Darling_River "Darling River"), such as the [Barwon](/wiki/Barwon_River_(New_South_Wales) "Barwon River (New South Wales)"), [Gwydir](/wiki/Gwydir_River "Gwydir River"), and [Namoi Rivers](/wiki/Namoi_River "Namoi River"), which rise in the [Great Dividing Range](/wiki/Great_Dividing_Range "Great Dividing Range") country to its east.

The region has one city, [Tamworth](/wiki/Tamworth,_New_South_Wales "Tamworth, New South Wales"). The North West Slopes also includes the towns of [Gunnedah](/wiki/Gunnedah "Gunnedah"), [Bingara](/wiki/Bingara "Bingara"), [Manilla](/wiki/Manilla,_New_South_Wales "Manilla, New South Wales"), [Boggabri](/wiki/Boggabri "Boggabri"), [Mungindi](/wiki/Mungindi "Mungindi"), [Narrabri](/wiki/Narrabri "Narrabri"), [Moree](/wiki/Moree,_New_South_Wales "Moree, New South Wales"), [Quirindi](/wiki/Quirindi "Quirindi") and [Wee Waa](/wiki/Wee_Waa "Wee Waa"); and many villages.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

It has traditionally been a major [wheat](/wiki/Wheat "Wheat")-growing area, but in recent decades irrigated crops, especially [cotton](/wiki/Cotton "Cotton"), have become significant. The primary exports are cotton, [coal](/wiki/Coal "Coal"), beef, lamb and pork, cereal and oilseed grains.

The North West region is traversed by the [New England Highway](/wiki/New_England_Highway "New England Highway"), the [Newell Highway](/wiki/Newell_Highway "Newell Highway"), the [Kamilaroi Highway](/wiki/Kamilaroi_Highway "Kamilaroi Highway"), the [Oxley Highway](/wiki/Oxley_Highway "Oxley Highway") and the [Gwydir Highway](/wiki/Gwydir_Highway "Gwydir Highway"); and by railways such as the [Main Western railway line](/wiki/Main_Western_railway_line,_New_South_Wales "Main Western railway line, New South Wales"), linking [Bourke](/wiki/Bourke,_New_South_Wales "Bourke, New South Wales") to [Sydney](/wiki/Sydney "Sydney") with connections to the port of [Newcastle](/wiki/Newcastle,_New_South_Wales "Newcastle, New South Wales"). The [Main North railway line](/wiki/Main_North_railway_line,_New_South_Wales "Main North railway line, New South Wales") trains no longer continue to the [Queensland](/wiki/Queensland "Queensland") border, but Werris Creek, Tamworth and [Kootingal](/wiki/Kootingal "Kootingal") are still served by the [NSW TrainLink](/wiki/NSW_TrainLink "NSW TrainLink") Xplorer service between Sydney and [Armidale](/wiki/Armidale "Armidale").

[Mount Kaputar National Park](/wiki/Mount_Kaputar_National_Park "Mount Kaputar National Park"), near Narrabri, is within the North West Slopes region.

This region is often included as part of the [New England](/wiki/New_England_(New_South_Wales) "New England (New South Wales)") region. Sometimes the region is also known as the **New England North West**[[3]](#cite_note-3) or less commonly the Northern Inland Region[[4]](#cite_note-4) when it also includes the Northern Tablelands.

[Local Government Areas](/wiki/Local_government_in_Australia "Local government in Australia") include [Gunnedah Shire](/wiki/Gunnedah_Shire "Gunnedah Shire"), [Liverpool Plains Shire](/wiki/Liverpool_Plains_Shire "Liverpool Plains Shire"), [Moree Plains Shire](/wiki/Moree_Plains_Shire "Moree Plains Shire"), [Narrabri Shire](/wiki/Narrabri_Shire "Narrabri Shire"), [Gwydir Shire](/wiki/Gwydir_Shire "Gwydir Shire") and [Tamworth Regional Council](/wiki/Tamworth_Regional_Council "Tamworth Regional Council").

## Climate

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=North_West_Slopes&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Climate")]

The climate here is at the mercy of the [Horse Latitudes](/wiki/Horse_Latitudes "Horse Latitudes") high pressure belt for the grand majority of the year. Thus a distinct summer rainfall peak is observed, in stark contrast to its more poleward counterpart, the [South West Slopes](/wiki/South_West_Slopes "South West Slopes") (wherein a spring or winter rainfall peak is observed). The seasonal range of maximum temperatures is also strikingly narrower than that observed in the South West Slopes.

Summers are long, stable and very hot, with severe thunderstorms and [supercells](/wiki/Supercells "Supercells") a frequent occurrence. Winters are considerably sunnier than those further south; cold by nightfall, though mild and sometimes even warm by day; constituting a great diurnal range and frequent, oftentimes heavy frost.

Snow is a very rare occurrence in the lowlands (snow has been recorded as low as [Gunnedah](/wiki/Gunnedah "Gunnedah")), though it occurs annually on high-points such as the [Nandewar Range](/wiki/Nandewar_Range "Nandewar Range"), [Warrumbungles](/wiki/Warrumbungles "Warrumbungles") and [Coolah Tops](/wiki/Coolah_Tops_National_Park "Coolah Tops National Park"). Even so, it is far from a regular occurrence on account of the region's northern latitude. In nearly all cases, a snowfall event this far north requires a [cut-off low](/wiki/Cut-off_low "Cut-off low") system: in which the coldest air bypasses the usual southern alpine regions, instead advancing in a north-northeasterly direction up the centre of New South Wales.
